title: Kidney Health. Long-Term Dietary Consumption of Grapes Affects Kidney Health in C57BL/6 J Mice. In mice consuming a grape-enriched diet for one year, beneficial impacts on kidney health were observed, compared to those on the standard diet. Those consuming the grape-enriched diet exhibited decreased body to kidney weight ratio; significantly reduced blood urea nitrogen which was associated with enhanced perfusion in the kidney that may have contributed to that protective effect; and beneficial up- and down-regulation of specific genes related to kidney health, immune health, and others, including improved mitochondrial function.

sentences.text: Long-Term Dietary Consumption of Grapes Affects Kidney Health in C57BL/6 J Mice.
In mice consuming a grape-enriched diet for one year, beneficial impacts on kidney health were observed, compared to those on the standard diet.

Those consuming the grape-enriched diet exhibited decreased body to kidney weight ratio; significantly reduced blood urea nitrogen which was associated with enhanced perfusion in the kidney that may have contributed to that protective effect; and beneficial up- and down-regulation of specific genes related to kidney health, immune health, and others, including improved mitochondrial function.
Multi-Organ Nutrigenomic Effects of Dietary Grapes in a Mouse Model.

In a year-long study where mice consumed a grape-enriched diet, eating the equivalent of 2.5 servings of grapes per day, study findings showed the ability of grapes to modulate gene expression in the kidney.

Consuming grapes enriched an array of beneficial pathways and down-regulated harmful pathways, resulting in enhanced immune regulators, repair potential of the kidneys, ability to fight infections, and suppressed genes related to carcinogenesis.
Daily Intake of Grape Powder Prevents the Progression of Kidney Disease in Obese Type 2 Diabetic ZSF1 Rats.

In a study using an animal model for metabolic syndrome-associated chronic kidney disease, consuming a 5 percent grape powder diet for 6 months protected renal function in obese, diabetic animals, preventing the progression of kidney disease.
The grape-enriched diet prevented kidney tissue damage by increasing antioxidant defense in the kidneys, upregulating antioxidant promoting genes and downregulating genes linked to oxidative stress.
Consumption of the grape diet also lowered serum glucose and improved serum protein status. status
